claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with scope field accepting project | external | user | unknown
evidence.why: This is the primary schema definition that evidence records must follow.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES is declared as Set containing exactly these four values
evidence.why: This constant is used for validation throughout the codebase.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope function validates scope against VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES
evidence.why: This validation ensures all evidence records declare a valid scope.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker when scope is missing or invalid
evidence.why: This enforces scope declaration as a hard requirement for readiness.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies records into project/external/user/unknown based on multiple heuristics
evidence.why: This shows the system can distinguish scopes even when not explicitly declared.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when existing-project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators
evidence.why: This enforces that project facts require project evidence with file paths.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions requiring scope declaration and distinguishing project from external evidence
evidence.why: This is the prompt that tells evidence agents to declare scopes correctly.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rule: Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown; Project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts, with a repo-relative path or file locator
evidence.why: This is the explicit instruction to agents about scope requirements.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates that each evidence record declares one of the four valid scopes
evidence.why: This runtime validation blocks results without proper scope declarations.

evidence.summary: Test proves external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing-project facts and generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker
evidence.why: This test verifies the system rejects external evidence for project facts.

evidence.summary: Test proves project evidence without concrete file locator generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: This test verifies project evidence requires a repo-relative path.

evidence.summary: Test proves external URLs mislabeled as project evidence are blocked with PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED
evidence.why: This test verifies URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence.

evidence.summary: Test proves evidence records without explicit scope declaration generate EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: This test verifies scope declaration is mandatory.

evidence.summary: Test verifies cross-check PRD readiness requires all three evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) to complete
evidence.why: This test verifies the evidence agent role requirements.

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error
evidence.why: This test verifies scope enforcement happens at runtime.

evidence.summary: Doctrine documents evidence scope requirements and evidence agent protocols with explicit scope rules
evidence.why: This is the primary documentation explaining the scope distinction design.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: evidence records must declare scope as project | external | user | unknown; project evidence requires repo-relative path; external evidence can only be background research
evidence.why: This documents the core contract for scope distinction.

risks: If this claim is wrong, existing-project factual claims could be accepted based on web research or user assertions without project code/test/docs evidence, leading to incorrect PRD generation

result.notes: All four components (schema, prompt, readiness contract, tests) explicitly distinguish evidence scopes with validation and blockers. The system enforces scope declaration and requires project-scoped evidence with file locators for existing-project facts.
